name: Seated Leg Curl
resistance_profile: Seated position holds the hip flexed, keeping the hamstrings at longer length under load than lying/standing curls — a lengthened-biased knee-flexion choice.
rom_notes: Allow the knees to extend fully into the stretch between reps; curl to full flexion.
cues: Fix the hips against the pad
Curl fully, then control the return to a stretch
Avoid using momentum or lifting the hips
execution_steps: Sit in the machine with the back pad supporting you and the lever resting above your ankles.
Secure the thigh pad across your legs and grip the handles.
Curl your heels down and under toward the seat as far as possible.
Return under control until your knees straighten and the hamstrings stretch.
good_when: You want lengthened-biased knee-flexion work, since the flexed hip keeps the hamstrings long.
You want an easy-to-load, low-fatigue isolation for high volume.
bad_when: You already do plenty of hip-flexed stretch work and want the shortened end (use a lying curl).
No machine is available (use a sliding or Nordic curl).
common_errors: Partial range at the contracted end
